title: Children's Play Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Children's Play Groups 33 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Deafness BEAVERTON SCHOOL DISTRICT 48 FAMILY RESOURCE COORDINATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Family Resource Coordinator (FRC) provides information and referrals to families about early learning, parenting support, basic needs, and health. Also coordinates play groups, parenting classes, and early learning workshops. The FRC is community-based and does not have an office, but rotates between elementary school locations. In classes, parents have the opportunity to interact with each other and boost their parenting skills while learning nurturing strategies to support their children at home and in school. Topics covered may include social and language skills, independence and problem-solving skills, self-care, resourcefulness, positive discipline, wellbeing, and financial literacy. Child care and snacks/meal provided. 1260 NW Waterhouse Avenue Beaverton, OR 97006 Eligibility: Families with children 0-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm. Groups and classes vary. Email (503) 277-0484 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES MINI MOUNTAINEERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects families with nature and each other. Provides weekly outings including trail hikes, park and play, kid-led walks, urban strolls, and nature scavenger hunts. 207 NE Park Street Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-5 Hours: Varies by outing Email (541) 426-9411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES EASTERN OREGON MINI MOUNTAINEERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects families with nature and each other. Provides weekly outings including trail hikes, park and play, kid-led walks, urban strolls, and nature scavenger hunts. 2725 7th Street Room 002 Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-5 Hours: Varies by outing (541) 524-2331 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DAISY CHAIN MUSIC CLASSES/BABY POP MUSIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers interactive music classes incorporating singing and American Sign Language (ASL) in a playgroup. 1270 Charnelton Street Daisy's Place 2nd Floor Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Ages 0-5 Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 505-1139 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY FAMILY RESOURCE CENTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Locator tool with list of Family Resource Centers (FRCs) by school district. Provides a range of services and support to families with children, including child literacy groups and story times, playgroups, assistance with accessing school personnel, parent education, parent literacy and computer support, and other drop-in services for families and children. Some locations may offer children's clothing and other supplies. 3171 Gateway Loop Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by site Email (541) 741-6000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY THE BLACK EARLY LEARNING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides developmental screening, referrals to educational and community resources, learning opportunities, connects children with other Black children, and supports parents as they help children learn and grow. 3171 Gateway Loop Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Ages 0-6 African American/Black children Hours: Varies Email (541) 357-5719 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY LEARNING HUB OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY CLACKAMAS EARLY LEARNING HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A network of providers who work to enhance the ability of parents and caregivers to stimulate, protect, and nurture children. In order to lower the incidences of child abuse and neglect, increase effective parenting, and improve family health and well-being via parent education trainings, strengthening linkages among providers, and improving access to support for all families. Coordinated enrollment into subsidized preschool, including Head Start and Preschool Promise. Serves as the Coordinate Early Learning Hub for the county. 821 Main Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-8 Families at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level for subsidized preschool enrollment Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (971) 288-8264 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE FAMILY YMCA COMMUNITY RECREATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of recreation opportunities, including an aquatic center, gym, group exercise classes, preschool, afterschool childcare, family fun events, and youth and adult sports. Also provides teen youth clubs and drop-in events. The Virtual Y Hub provides on-demand videos for fitness, yoga, meditation, and more that people can watch at home. 600 E 24th Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Unrestricted, some programs require YMCA membership Hours: Varies by site Email (541) 686-9622 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY TODDLER TUMMY TIME PLAYGROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly playgroup for parents and caregivers to come together and learn learn about child development, how to build early attachments, and how to work through parenting struggles. 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Parents or caregivers with babies age 0-9 months Hours: Tuesday 1:45pm-3:45pm Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOREST GROVE SCHOOL DISTRICT EARLY CHILDHOOD FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER FAMILY RESOURCE MANAGER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one information and referrals to families about early learning, parenting support, basic needs, and health. Provides information about and hosts play groups, parenting classes, and early learning workshops. In class, parents have an opportunity to interact with each other and boost their parenting skills while learning nurturing strategies to support their children at home and in school. 200 N 14th Avenue Cornelius, OR 97113 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 359-8817 5504 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOUR RIVERS EARLY LEARNING AND PARENTING EARLY LEARNING HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers collaborative programs and systems for kindergarten readiness, as well as community events, children's playgroups, and classes for parents and guardians of young children. 400 E Scenic Drive Suite 207 The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Ages 0-5 children and their parent or guardian Hours: Varies Email (541) 993-2396 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FRIENDLY HOUSE CHILDREN'S PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides after-school enrichment program with activities such as art, science, homework help, sports, field trips, and playtime. Children have the opportunity to participate in weekly clubs like photography, theater, gardening, and art. Day camps are available during school breaks. 1737 NW 26th Avenue Portland, OR 97210 Eligibility: After-school program: K-5 grade students of Chapman Elementary Summer Camp: 1-6 grade students Winter Camp: K-5 grade students Spring Break Camp: K-5 grade students Hours: After-school program: Monday-Friday 7am-7:50am and 2:30pm-6pm Summer Camp: 9am-4pm; optional before care (7am-9am) and aftercare (4pm-6pm) Winter Camp: 7:30am-6pm Spring Break Camp: 7:30am-6pm Email (503) 228-4391 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLADSTONE CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting materials, family events, and a free lending library. It also hosts a variety of classes and groups offered by the agencies within the center. The agencies it houses include the Gladstone Health & Wellness Center, pediatric health, emotional and dental services. Family Stepping Stones, Relief Nursery, Head Start and Early Head Start, Northwest Family services navigators, Clackamas Education Service District EI/ECSE program, LifeWorks Northwest family counseling, WIC, SMART, Clackamas Community College GED program, and the Gladstone School District kindergarten program. 18905 Portland Avenue Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Households with children from birth through kindergarten Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm, also evening hours vary Email (503) 496-3939 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GRANTS PASS SCHOOL DISTRICT 7 PARENT AND CHILD PLAYGROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers community activities, parent workshops, library visits, story time, and exciting crafts to enriching field trips, informative Food Hero nutrition classes, and health and dental pre-screening services. 725 North East Dean Drive Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Children ages 3-5 years old Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 9am-11am Email (541) 474-5709 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GROWING TOGETHER FAMILY COMMUNITY CENTER PLAY GROUPS AND CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of early childhood education and play groups for children. Also offers classes for adult parenting skills. 310 SW H Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Varies by class. Most classes are for children ages 0-5 and their caregivers. Hours: Varies by class Email (541) 864-0776 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO NETWORK P3 COORDINATORS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides play groups, kindergarten readiness, and leadership development (focusing on parents' engagement within the school setting). 410 NE 18th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: -Play and Learn Groups: Pregnant individuals in any trimester and ages 0-5 -Early Kindergarten Transition (EKT)/Parent Cafe: Must have a child who will enter or attend a participating school Hours: Varies by event Email (503) 283-6881 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER PARENTING GROUPS AND CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides childbirth education, newborn care classes, safe sleep workshop, breastfeeding workshop/support, and other classes which vary by season. 1515 NE 41st Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Most classes are for parents and other caregivers of children under the age of 5 Hours: Class times vary Email (503) 249-5801 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PDX PARENT PDXPARENT.COM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about parent support groups, children's playgroups, and family activities. Offers a community events calendar, and magazine as well. Website Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 460-2774 Visit Website More Details PROVIDENCE HEALTH AND SERVICES OREGON AND SOUTHWEST WASHINGTON SWINDELLS RESOURCE CENTER HELP ME GROW OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers supports for families, especially those who experience behavioral, developmental, and complex medical concerns. Connects parents and caregivers to parent education classes, sibling support groups, and inclusive developmental playgroups. 9135 SW Barnes Road Portland, OR 97225 Eligibility: Families with children 0-5 years and pregnant individuals in any trimester Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (883) 868-4769 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROLLING HILLS COMMUNITY CHURCH POP IN PLAY DATE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a faith-based play group. 3550 SW Borland Road Tualatin, OR 97062 Eligibility: Ages 0-5 and their parents or caregivers Hours: Thursday 10am-noon. Service hours may vary. Email (503) 208-5599 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COAST REGIONAL EARLY LEARNING HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support for school readiness, including kindergarten preparation programs. Family and parenting resources are available as well 519 South 7th Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by program Email (458) 710-0902 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH LANE SCHOOL DISTRICT THE EARLY LEARNING CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supporting families with children to be successful in school by providing, and connecting to programs, and services that advance early literacy, promote health, and development, and foster strong resilient relationships. Also offers parent-child interaction play-groups known as Peggy's Primary Connection. 1310 S 8th Street Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Varies. (Peggy's Primary Connection 0-5 years old) Hours: Varies by service (541) 942-3532 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHERN OREGON EARLY LEARNING SERVICES HUB READ PLAY TALKGROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in, early learning play group. 310 Southwest H Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Preschool-age children and their parents or caregivers. Younger siblings welcome at meetings. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm (541) 776-8590 1145 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST JOHNS SWAPNPLAY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community space for families to play and connect. With 7 unique indoor and outdoor play zones focused on supporting healthy childhood development, it also offers 2 "swapping" rooms where items like books, toys, gear, and other items can be donated and shared. Other services include parent-child activities, parent networking opportunities, meet-ups, and classes. 7535 N Chicago Avenue Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-7, older siblings welcome Hours: Monday-Saturday by appointment Email (971) 251-0074 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TIGARD-TUALATIN FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER EARLY LEARNING COORDINATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referrals to families about early learning, preschool programs, parenting support, basic needs, health and information, and referral to community partners. 9550 SW Murdock Street Tigard, OR 97224 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 or pregnant individuals Hours: By appointment only. Class times and locations vary. EARLY INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Focuses on developing collaborative partnerships with parents and children that facilitate spoken language development through listening. Offers intensive auditory-oral intervention, on-site audiologist who does hearing aid analysis and cochlear implant mapping, regular home and school sessions, facilitated toddler groups with both children with typical hearing and children who are deaf and support and information for families. 2860 SE Holgate Boulevard Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Newborns to age 3 with a hearing impairment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 235-6551 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED WAY OF LANE COUNTY EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting resources and classes, a calendar of community and family events, and a variety of topical and age-based articles on parenting, and more. 3171 Gateway Loop Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies depending on classes or events Email (541) 741-6000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WILDFLOWER PLAY COLLECTIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers an indoor playground. Memberships include daily use of the playspace, weekly classes, events, access to the WildFlower Swap Closet, gently used books, toys, clothes, and gear. Donations are accepted. 58751 S Bachelor Flat Road Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Ages 0-6 years old Hours: Saturday-Sunday 7am-7pm Email (503) 664-0669 Get Directions Visit Website More Details
